description: Create a native SwiftUI iOS app for Gather Lists — Phase 1: Xcode project setup, Supabase Swift SDK integration, full auth flow (Apple Sign-In + email/password), and authenticated tab bar shell.

credentialRequirements.fallbackPlan: Cannot proceed without — same URL and anon key values as React app .env
Same team and capability already configured for Capacitor app

userStories.title: Create Xcode Project & Folder Structure
userStories.description: Create the native SwiftUI project inside the monorepo at ios-native/ with proper folder structure, bundle ID, deployment target, and device orientation settings.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Xcode project is created at ios-native/GatherLists/GatherLists.xcodeproj using SwiftUI App lifecycle (no storyboards, no UIKit AppDelegate)
Bundle ID: com.gatherlists
Deployment target: iOS 17.0
Swift version: 5.10+
Device orientation: Portrait only (iPhone)
The project compiles and runs on iOS Simulator showing a placeholder 'Hello, Gather Lists!' screen

Folder structure follows the specified pattern: GatherListsApp.swift, Config/, Models/, Services/, ViewModels/, Views/ (with Auth/, Lists/, Recipes/, Stores/, Settings/, Components/ subdirs), Assets.xcassets, Info.plist, GatherLists.entitlements, Preview Content/
Add .gitignore entries for ios-native/ Xcode artifacts (*.xcuserdata, DerivedData/, build/, etc.) if not already covered by the repo's root .gitignore
xcodebuild -project ios-native/GatherLists/GatherLists.xcodeproj -scheme GatherLists -sdk iphonesimulator -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16' build succeeds
userStories.notes: Xcode project created at ios-native/GatherLists/ with SwiftUI App lifecycle, bundle ID com.gatherlists, iOS 17.0 deployment target, portrait only. Build succeeds on iPhone 16e simulator.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Project scaffolding — build success is the only verification needed

userStories.title: Integrate Supabase Swift SDK & Configuration
userStories.description: Add the Supabase Swift SDK via SPM and configure the client with credentials loaded from a git-ignored Secrets.plist.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase Swift SDK (https://github.com/supabase/supabase-swift.git) added as a Swift Package Manager dependency, version 2.x (latest stable)
A Config/Secrets.plist file holds SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY values (same as the React .env)
Secrets.plist is added to .gitignore so credentials are never committed
A Secrets.plist.example file exists with placeholder values for onboarding
A Services/SupabaseManager.swift singleton (or similar) initializes the Supabase client from Secrets.plist values
The client is accessible app-wide (via singleton, environment object, or dependency injection)
The project builds successfully with the SDK integrated
A simple smoke test: on app launch, print the Supabase client status or attempt an anonymous health check to verify connectivity
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: SDK integration and config — build success plus connectivity smoke test

userStories.title: Auth State Management & Session Persistence
userStories.description: Build the authentication state layer using @Observable that gates the entire app behind login, with session persistence across app launches.
acceptanceCriteria: An AuthViewModel (or AuthManager) using @Observable manages auth state: isLoading, isAuthenticated, currentUser, error
On app launch, the view model checks for an existing session via supabase.auth.session — if a valid session exists, the user is authenticated immediately (no login screen flash)
Auth state changes are observed via supabase.auth.authStateChanges stream
Session auto-refresh is enabled (via SDK defaults or explicit startAutoRefresh())
The root GatherListsApp.swift conditionally renders: loading spinner while isLoading is true, login view when isAuthenticated is false, main tab view when isAuthenticated is true
A signOut() method clears the session and returns to the login view
The auth state persists across app launches (SDK's default keychain storage)
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Auth state logic with session persistence and conditional rendering

userStories.title: Apple Sign-In Flow
userStories.description: Implement native Apple Sign-In using AuthenticationServices framework, passing the ID token to Supabase for authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: The GatherLists.entitlements file includes the com.apple.developer.applesignin capability (same as existing Capacitor app)
The login view shows a 'Sign in with Apple' button using SwiftUI's SignInWithAppleButton or a custom styled button matching the Gather Lists brand
Tapping the button triggers the native ASAuthorizationController flow
On success, the Apple ID token is passed to Supabase via supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken(credentials: .init(provider: .apple, idToken: idToken, nonce: nonce))
A SHA256-hashed nonce is generated for each sign-in attempt and verified by Supabase
On success, the app transitions to the authenticated tab view
On failure, an error message is displayed to the user
The sign-in works on a physical device (requires Apple Developer team signing)
The Apple Sign-In associates with the same Supabase user as the React app's Apple Sign-In (same provider, same Apple ID → same auth.users row)
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Native auth flow with nonce generation — requires physical device for full verification

userStories.title: Email/Password Sign-In & Sign-Up
userStories.description: Implement email/password authentication matching the React app's login form, with sign-in, sign-up, form validation, and brand styling.
acceptanceCriteria: The login view has a toggle or tab to switch between Apple Sign-In and email/password
Email/password form has: email field, password field, 'Sign In' button, 'Sign Up' link/toggle
Sign-in calls supabase.auth.signIn(email:password:)
Sign-up calls supabase.auth.signUp(email:password:)
On success, the app transitions to the authenticated tab view
On failure, a clear error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password', 'Account already exists')
Form validation: email format check, password minimum length (Supabase default: 6 chars)
The keyboard dismisses on submit or tap outside
The login view is visually branded: Gather Lists logo/icon at top, brand colors (#B5E8C8 → #A8D8EA gradient, text #3D7A63), Nunito font if available or system font as fallback
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Form validation, error handling, and branded UI

userStories.title: Authenticated Tab Bar Shell
userStories.description: Build the main TabView that authenticated users see, with 4 tabs (Lists, Recipes, Stores, Settings) and placeholder content, plus a working sign-out button.
acceptanceCriteria: After authentication, the user sees a TabView with 4 tabs: Lists, Recipes, Stores, Settings
Each tab has an SF Symbol icon: Lists (list.bullet or checklist), Recipes (book or fork.knife), Stores (storefront or building.2), Settings (gearshape)
Each tab shows a placeholder view with the tab name (e.g., 'Lists — Coming Soon') so it's clear the shell works
Tab bar uses the Gather Lists primary color (#3D7A63) for the selected tab tint
The Settings tab includes a working 'Sign Out' button that calls signOut() and returns to the login view
The Settings tab displays the current user's email and display name (from supabase.auth.session.user)
The tab bar supports both light and dark mode (uses system appearance with custom accent color)
The app builds and runs on iOS Simulator successfully
xcodebuild build succeeds

userStories.title: App Icon, Launch Screen & Entitlements
userStories.description: Configure the app's visual identity (icon, launch screen with brand logo) and entitlements (Apple Sign-In, Associated Domains) to match the existing App Store listing.
acceptanceCriteria: The app icon in Assets.xcassets uses the existing Gather Lists icon (copy from ios/App/App/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/ or regenerate from public/logo/icon.svg)
The launch screen shows the Gather Lists stacked logo (icon + name + tagline) on a white background, matching the existing Capacitor app's splash screen
Dark mode launch screen variant uses #1a1a2e background with white text (matching existing)
Entitlements file includes: com.apple.developer.applesignin → ['Default'], com.apple.developer.associated-domains → ['applinks:gatherlists.com']
Info.plist includes: CFBundleDisplayName 'Gather Lists', CFBundleURLTypes with URL scheme 'gather' and identifier 'com.gatherlists', UISupportedInterfaceOrientations Portrait only
Privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) matches the existing Capacitor app's declarations
Build succeeds and the app icon is visible in Simulator's home screen
xcodebuild build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Asset configuration — build success and visual verification
